title: what should I buy
wikitext:
[]: what should I buy?             Quote Post by thomas » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:09 am Shuld I will buy the "Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Collection" or "Neon Genesis Evangelion Perfect Collection"? Will somebody please explan to me the difference between them?... :) Neon Genesis Evangelion Perfect Collection [url=http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Platinum-Collection/dp/B000767QTA/ref=imdbpov_dvd_4/002-4131022-8120056?ie=UTF8&qid=1175959098&sr=8-2[/b]] Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Collection [/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:18 am Platinum Collection: Remastered Visuals - much more crisp, drastically reduced frame-shake. 5.1 Surround sound. Better subtitle translations throughout. Cheaper than Perfect Collection. Perfect Collection - None of the Above. For others who read this: Did anyone else notice that the only episode in Platinum that still seemed to have frame shake was ep. 7 (That Which is Manmade)? Much of it seemed like they had forgot to remaster it. It's really strange because the frame shake is practically gone throughout every other episode but in several parts of ep. 7 it's still really bad. When I watched mine I actually wondered if my disc was defective...

[]:             Quote Post by thomas » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:36 am there are all of the episodes at the Platinum Collection and also at the Perfect Collection? If the answer is "yes", so why in the Perfect Collection there are 2 more discs? are the movies in the Perfect Collection?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:54 am The Platinum collection contains all the episodes plus the director's cuts of ep. 21-24 (Perfect doesn't have the director's cuts). Neither contain Death & Rebirth or End of Evangelion. There IS no reason to buy the Perfect Collection. I don't know why it's still for sale.

[]:             Quote Post by thomas » Sat Apr 07, 2007 1:33 pm thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 07, 2007 1:45 pm Also, be careful on what you ask and how you ask it. Just some friendly advice...

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sat Apr 07, 2007 1:56 pm Is death and rebirth worth the buy? Just curious... sorry for hijacking.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Apr 07, 2007 2:08 pm Death is pretty interesting. It is a recap of the series up to episode 24. Rebirth is just the first 20 minutes of End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 07, 2007 2:36 pm I wrote this for nothing, didn't I?

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 07, 2007 3:37 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I wrote this for nothing, didn't I?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 07, 2007 4:14 pm If you've seen all 24 episodes of NGE plus the director's cuts of 21-24, I think Death is pretty pointless. IMO it's a pretty muddled recap of the series with not much interesting to add once you strip away the "new" scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sat Apr 07, 2007 4:46 pm Appreciate it all.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Apr 07, 2007 4:51 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: If you've seen all 24 episodes of NGE plus the director's cuts of 21-24, I think Death is pretty pointless. IMO it's a pretty muddled recap of the series with not much interesting to add once you strip away the "new" scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 07, 2007 5:58 pm ...And Shinji is still fucked up! Thank you for this public service annoucement.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 07, 2007 10:25 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Death is pretty pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:11 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: What about those days when you want to watch The End of Evangelion and get the full effect of watching it right after the series and don't have 13 hours to watch the entire series?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:16 pm I thought Death was refreshingly well done for a recap. A lot more interesting that your usual recaps, which starts from what happened in the beginning and tells you bits and peices until you get to the end. I liked how they focused on a character at a time, until the entire story is weaved together in a new way.

[]:             Quote Post by thomas » Sun Apr 08, 2007 2:11 am how much minutes is Death & Rebirth? and this buy is worthwhile?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 08, 2007 3:05 am thomas wrote: how much minutes is Death & Rebirth? and this buy is worthwhile?

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sun Apr 08, 2007 4:02 am I usually buy stuff from DeepDiscount.com because it is often cheaper than Amazon and they have free domestic shipping (not to mention substantially discounted overseas shipping for any of you overseas people). Sometimes they even have sales that let you pick stuff up quite cheap.
summary: []: what should I buy?             Quote Post by thomas » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:09 am Shuld I will buy the "Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Collection" or "Neon Genesis Evangelion Perfect Collection"?
